note: D4-P0-001 stays P0 but is now fully characterized: systemic + realized-data-corruption (213 live) + currently-inert-consumer-surface (architecturally decoupled: resume/phase-map/completion-verification/memory-search all bypass derived.status) + feasible-fix (holds 403 TP rate). The blast-radius result is NOT a downgrade (downgradeTrigger required deriveStatus patch OR per-folder audit showing 213 genuinely-complete). The corruption is latent data-integrity risk, not today's functional failure. Remediation urgency: land the fix before a new consumer (dashboard/rollup/search-boost) inherits the signal. The strict validator is structurally blind (enum-valid). Iter-7 recommended: surface the validator cross-field-gap as a D4 sibling finding, and count the pure-null completion_pct subset to size the fix's edge case.

reasoning: Exhaustive consumer grep + source-read proves no user-facing consumer branches on derived.status today. The 213 corrupted rows are real (iter-5 natural experiment) but inert (decoupled consumers). Severity P0 stands on data-integrity grounds (repo-wide false data + validator-blind + non-contractual decoupling). Minimal deriveStatus fix is feasible and preserves the 403 true-positive rate. D3 zero-drift re-confirmed. stop_policy=max-iterations -> iteration 7 broadens to the validator cross-field consistency gap (sibling to D1-P2-001).
